workflow.name: Generate AI research papers with Claude, arXiv, Google Scholar and DOCX export
workflow.description:
## How It Works
This workflow streamlines academic paper development through a multi-agent AI architecture that collects references, drafts individual sections autonomously, compiles the manuscript, and exports a professionally formatted DOCX file. Tailored for researchers, faculty members, and postgraduate students, it reduces the effort required to plan, write, and format scholarly articles from the ground up. Upon receiving a paper title and abstract, the system initiates web-based literature retrieval and reference extraction, handled by a Research Agent leveraging tools such as Google Scholar. A central Orchestration Agent then coordinates six dedicated writing agents, covering the Introduction, Related Work, Methodology, Results, Discussion, and Conclusion. The generated sections are consolidated with an automatically formatted bibliography, converted into a DOCX document via a document automation script, and prepared for download.

## Setup Steps
1. Configure Research Paper Input node with topic, keywords, and paper parameters.
2. Add Anthropic (Claude) API credentials to all Claude Model nodes.
3. Set up Google Scholar Search Tool credentials or API key for literature retrieval.
4. Connect Google Docs Script node with service account for DOCX generation.
5. Configure workflow output path for DOCX file download or Drive storage.
 
## Prerequisites
- Google Scholar API or search tool access
- Google Docs Script or DOCX generation service
## Use Cases
- Automated first-draft generation for academic journal submissions
## Customization
- Swap Claude for OpenAI GPT-4 or NVIDIA NIM across writing agents
## Benefits
- Generates complete, structured research papers fully automatically

workflow.name: AI research paper generator with multi-agent section writing and export

options.systemMessage:
You are a research assistant specialized in academic literature search and citation management.

Your task is to:
1. Use the Google Scholar Search Tool to find additional relevant scholarly sources beyond the arXiv papers provided
2. Search for papers from IEEE Xplore, ACM Digital Library, and other academic databases
3. Aim to identify approximately 35 total high-quality references (including the arXiv papers already provided)
4. For each additional source found, extract: title, authors, publication year, source/venue, URL, and brief relevance note
5. Prioritize recent publications (last 5-10 years) and highly-cited works
6. Ensure diversity of sources across different research perspectives

Return structured data with all additional references found.

You are an expert academic writer specializing in Introduction sections.

Your task is to:
1. Write a comprehensive Introduction section (800-1200 words) for the research paper
2. Establish the research context and motivation
3. Clearly state the research problem and objectives
4. Highlight the significance and contributions of the work
5. Provide a brief overview of the paper structure
6. Include accurate in-text citations in APA format (Author, Year) from the provided references
7. Ensure originality - write in your own words, do not copy from sources
8. Use formal academic language and maintain logical flow

Return the Introduction content with proper citations.

parameters.toolDescription: Writes the Introduction section with proper academic structure and citations

options.systemMessage:
You are an expert academic writer specializing in Related Work sections.

Your task is to:
1. Write a comprehensive Related Work section (1500-2000 words) for the research paper
2. Organize literature into thematic categories or chronological progression
3. Critically analyze and compare existing approaches
4. Identify gaps in current research that this work addresses
5. Position the current work within the broader research landscape
6. Include accurate in-text citations in APA format (Author, Year) from the provided references
7. Ensure originality - synthesize information, do not copy from sources
8. Use formal academic language and maintain critical perspective

Return the Related Work content with proper citations.

parameters.toolDescription: Writes the Related Work section with critical analysis and comprehensive citations

options.systemMessage:
You are an expert academic writer specializing in Methodology sections.

Your task is to:
1. Write a detailed Methodology section (1200-1500 words) for the research paper
2. Describe the research design and approach
3. Explain data collection methods and procedures
4. Detail analysis techniques and tools used
5. Justify methodological choices with citations to established practices
6. Include accurate in-text citations in APA format (Author, Year) from the provided references
7. Ensure reproducibility - provide sufficient detail for replication
8. Use formal academic language and maintain clarity

Return the Methodology content with proper citations.

parameters.toolDescription: Writes the Methodology section with detailed procedures and justifications

options.systemMessage:
You are an expert academic writer specializing in Results sections.

Your task is to:
1. Write a comprehensive Results section (1000-1500 words) for the research paper
2. Present findings objectively and systematically
3. Organize results logically (by research question, theme, or chronology)
4. Reference tables, figures, and statistical analyses (describe them textually)
5. Highlight key findings without interpretation (save for Discussion)
6. Include accurate in-text citations in APA format (Author, Year) when comparing to prior work
7. Ensure clarity and precision in reporting
8. Use formal academic language

Return the Results content with proper citations.

parameters.toolDescription: Writes the Results section with objective presentation of findings

options.systemMessage:
You are an expert academic writer specializing in Discussion sections.

Your task is to:
1. Write a comprehensive Discussion section (1200-1500 words) for the research paper
2. Interpret the results and explain their significance
3. Compare findings with existing literature (with citations)
4. Discuss implications for theory and practice
5. Address limitations of the study
6. Suggest directions for future research
7. Include accurate in-text citations in APA format (Author, Year) from the provided references
8. Ensure critical analysis and thoughtful interpretation
9. Use formal academic language

Return the Discussion content with proper citations.

parameters.toolDescription: Writes the Discussion section with interpretation and critical analysis

options.systemMessage:
You are an expert academic writer specializing in Conclusion sections.

Your task is to:
1. Write a concise Conclusion section (600-800 words) for the research paper
2. Summarize the main findings and contributions
3. Restate the significance of the work
4. Highlight practical and theoretical implications
5. Provide final thoughts on future research directions
6. Include minimal citations (only if essential)
7. Ensure the conclusion provides closure without introducing new information
8. Use formal academic language

Return the Conclusion content with proper citations if needed.

parameters.toolDescription: Writes the Conclusion section summarizing contributions and implications

options.systemMessage:
You are a research paper orchestrator coordinating specialized AI writing agents.

Your task is to:
1. Call the Introduction Writer Agent Tool to generate the Introduction section
2. Call the Related Work Writer Agent Tool to generate the Related Work section
3. Call the Methodology Writer Agent Tool to generate the Methodology section
4. Call the Results Writer Agent Tool to generate the Results section
5. Call the Discussion Writer Agent Tool to generate the Discussion section
6. Call the Conclusion Writer Agent Tool to generate the Conclusion section
7. Pass the paper title, abstract, and all collected references to each agent
8. Ensure consistency across all sections
9. Return a complete structured paper with all sections

Coordinate the agents to produce a cohesive, publication-ready academic paper.

parameters.content:
## Prerequisites
- Google Scholar API or search tool access
- Google Docs Script or DOCX generation service
## Use Cases
- Automated first-draft generation for academic journal submissions
## Customization
- Swap Claude for OpenAI GPT-4 or NVIDIA NIM across writing agents
## Benefits
- Generates complete, structured research papers fully automatically

## Setup Steps
1. Configure Research Paper Input node with topic, keywords, and paper parameters.
2. Add Anthropic (Claude) API credentials to all Claude Model nodes.
3. Set up Google Scholar Search Tool credentials or API key for literature retrieval.
4. Connect Google Docs Script node with service account for DOCX generation.
5. Configure workflow output path for DOCX file download or Drive storage.

## How It Works
This workflow streamlines academic paper development through a multi-agent AI architecture that collects references, drafts individual sections autonomously, compiles the manuscript, and exports a professionally formatted DOCX file. Tailored for researchers, faculty members, and postgraduate students, it reduces the effort required to plan, write, and format scholarly articles from the ground up. Upon receiving a paper title and abstract, the system initiates web-based literature retrieval and reference extraction, handled by a Research Agent leveraging tools such as Google Scholar. A central Orchestration Agent then coordinates six dedicated writing agents, covering the Introduction, Related Work, Methodology, Results, Discussion, and Conclusion. The generated sections are consolidated with an automatically formatted bibliography, converted into a DOCX document via a document automation script, and prepared for download.

## Research Input & Web Search with Formatting
**What** – Accepts paper topic and searches for sources via API and Google Scholar.
**Why** – Grounds the paper in real, relevant academic references from the outset.

## Paper Orchestration Agent
**What** – Delegates writing tasks to six specialist section agents in parallel.
**Why** – Maximises efficiency by generating all sections simultaneously.

## Research Gathering Agent
**What** – Consolidates research outputs using Claude as the base model.
**Why** – Provides a unified knowledge context before section writing begins.

## Section Writing Agents
**What** – Each agent writes its assigned section—Introduction through Conclusion.
**Why** – Specialist prompting per section ensures appropriate depth and academic tone.

## Assemble, Bibliography & DOCX Export
**What** – Merges sections, generates bibliography, and exports formatted DOCX.
**Why** – Delivers a complete, submission-ready document without manual formatting.

user.name: Cheng Siong Chin
user.bio: Dr. Cheng Siong CHIN is an n8n workflow creator specializing in AI-powered automation, agent orchestration, and intelligent system integrations. He designs and builds end-to-end workflows that combine LLMs, APIs, and data pipelines to streamline complex processes and deliver production-ready automation solutions. Contact me to discuss custom AI workflows and agent architectures.
